summary: Backend + frontend end-to-end testing of Castify Netflix-clone IPTV app. Verified all 5 required backend endpoints (/api/health, /api/status, /api/channels with include_german on/off, /api/hero, /api/refresh). Frontend UI verified: brand, hero, 5 rails, German toggle (default ON), search filter, nav click, channel card modal open, close via button and ESC.

minor.issue: First cold-connection request via public ingress timed out at 15s (retry immediately succeeded in 0.13s). Likely ingress cold-start, not a backend bug.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py L82 `abs(hash(line))` uses Python's non-deterministic string hash — channel IDs change across process restarts, breaking any client-side favorite/history persistence. Recommend using hashlib.md5(url).hexdigest()[:16] for stable IDs.
server.py: /api/refresh accepts no auth and can be called by anyone to invalidate the DB and trigger a 40s probe. Consider a simple shared-secret or rate limit for public deployments.
server.py L189-192: DB is cleared BEFORE inserting new working set. If insert_many fails mid-flight, DB is empty. Consider inserting into a temp collection then renaming, or writing atomically.
server.py: probe_status is a module-level global mutable dict — fine for single-worker uvicorn but will not sync across multiple workers.

App.js polling: when state transitions from probing to done, fetchChannels + fetchHero fire again, but the interval also fires an additional fetchChannels during 'done' iteration on the next tick. Minor extra request, not a bug.
VideoPlayerModal: hls.destroy runs on unmount but the underlying MediaSource blob URL is not revoked; low impact but a small memory leak on repeated open/close.

success_rate.backend: 100% (8/8 tests pass, 1 transient timeout on first run)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None — backend auto-seeds from public M3U playlists on startup (~1529 working channels after probing).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refresh test triggers a real 40s probe cycle — plan for it. Old DB channels remain visible during probing (DB is cleared only at the end of the new probe). Public URL from frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is the only entry point; backend is on port 8001 internally, routed via /api/*. Hero returns '24 Hour Free Movies (720p)' currently. HLS playback in headless Chromium yields the 'Stream unavailable' error overlay for most streams — this is expected behavior and the app handles it gracefully with the player-error state.
rca of the issue: No production bugs found. The one intermittent test failure (health timeout) was a transient network condition — the endpoint responded in 0.13s on retry.
